segments.text: I mean, when people ask questions like this, you know, about being bisexual and stuff like that, does that ever get in the way? No. No. No. Of course it doesn't. It's bloody fascinating. So when you take out a man, compared to when you take out a woman, do you feel like you treat him the same? Well, yeah. I think so.
Hey, good to meet you. Hey, you too. Shall we? Just a beer, please. Which one, sir?
Not bothered. The red wine. Which one, sir? The nice. Oh, the cheap one. It's on me. I insist. I halved it. I think the couple wanted to plant a trio. No way.

What did you say? No, because that's not what I'm after. What did you say? Oh, yeah, but I felt bad after. You want to find a bar? Yeah, that'd be great. Do you want to find a gay bar? Yeah, sure thing. My father took my hand and said, we may not live in the same world, but I'll always be by your side. I'll always be in your corner.

And I love you. Sorry, what about you? They found my porn and everybody cried. I think my parents always knew I was straight. It's never come up. It's a really weird question though, why would you ask me that? Give us a smile, love. Oh my god, I am so sorry about that. Give us a smile, love.
He was talking to me. I had a great time. Me too. I just...
You wanna come inside? Alright. Come in. It's been one minute.
How was that date the other night then, mate? Yeah, it was alright. Probably won't see him again. Oh, that's a shame. I'm sure you'll find someone. Yeah. I know. Have a look at her, mate. Look.
She's into meditation, wood carving and kite flying.
